hypothesis: Surface rules (NO-specific lede, CMI 4-stage, named staff proof, mandatory proof in every section) generalise to OEM brand/dealer pages — where reader pain is warranty voiding risk (rational/contractual) rather than seasonal deadline pressure (visceral). Evidence: blog 9/10, FAQ 9/10, prose service page 9/10. Hypothesis: same rules score 9/10 on a brand/dealer page explaining OEM authorisation scope and why factory service matters. Tests whether CMI structure works for trust-based/rational reader pain, not just urgency-based pain. No surface changes.
learning: OEM brand/dealer page format validated at 9/10, matching blog/FAQ/prose service page ceiling. Hypothesis confirmed: CMI 4-stage structure works for rational/contractual pain (warranty voiding risk) as well as urgency-based seasonal pain. Specific breakdown — NO-specific lede: Lake Superior 82,000 km² is strong, verifiable, geographically anchored (Lake Superior > all freshwater lakes on earth). CMI 4-stage clear in prose: warranty voiding risk (pain) → authorised dealer status (solution) → Lee/Damian/Cody factory-trained + Lynn OEM parts + Aaron written estimate (proof) → 'didn't buy here' objection answered explicitly (objection+CTA). Named staff in all 4 prose sections. Proof in every section confirmed. The 'If You Didn't Buy Here' section is the strongest objection-handling piece in any experiment to date — directly addresses the primary barrier for dealer-specific content and provides a clear path (serial number + model year to confirm eligibility). Canadian English throughout. One deduction: em-dash in the lede sentence ('82,000 square kilometres — more surface area') is a global rules violation. CTA is slightly generic ('call our service desk') without a real person or number. Four written web content formats now at 9/10 ceiling: blog (exp_3), FAQ (exp_8), prose service page (exp_9), OEM brand/dealer page (exp_10). Remaining gap to 10/10 is not format-specific — it appears structural: body sections are proof-strong but local-light after the lede.
